reasoning_model_1: The user explicitly requested the audio to be spoken at an 'extremely slow speed' (极慢速). Upon analyzing Model 1's audio (00:00 - 00:08), the total duration is approximately 8 seconds for the provided text. The speech rate is standard, conversational, and fluent (e.g., '我们每个人都应该深思熟虑' flows quickly between 00:00 and 00:03). While the content accuracy and audio fidelity are high, the model completely failed to adhere to the specific paralinguistic instruction regarding '语速' (speech rate). It did not slow down the speech at all, resulting in a delivery that contradicts the core requirement of the prompt.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2's audio (00:00 - 00:17) demonstrates a strict adherence to the 'extremely slow speed' instruction. The total duration is roughly 17 seconds, which is more than double the length of Model 1 for the same text. The speaker deliberately elongates syllables and incorporates significant pauses between phrases (e.g., '深思...熟虑' spanning 00:04 to 00:06). This pacing effectively creates the requested 'extremely slow' style while maintaining a natural, pondering tone suitable for the philosophical content of the text. The execution is distinct and fully compliant with the prompt.
model_comparison: The evaluation focuses on the controllable feature '语速' (Speech Rate), specifically the request for 'extremely slow' speech. Model 1 ignored this constraint entirely, delivering the text at a normal, fast-paced conversational speed (~8 seconds). In stark contrast, Model 2 successfully controlled the speech rate, stretching the delivery to ~17 seconds with deliberate pacing and pauses that perfectly matched the 'extremely slow' description. Since Model 1 failed to implement the requested feature while Model 2 implemented it perfectly, Model 2 is the clear winner.
